questions.text: Who is the main character who visits her aunt's house in the story?
questions.explanation: Mridu is a young girl living with her grandmother Tapi and grandfather Thatha in Chennai.

questions.text: What did Ravi show Mridu in the backyard?
questions.explanation: Ravi and Meena found a kitten outside the gate that morning and kept it hidden in the backyard.

questions.text: What was the name Ravi gave to the kitten?
questions.explanation: The full name Ravi gave was Mahendravarma Pallava Poonai (M.P. Poonai for short).

questions.text: Ravi claimed that the kitten was a descendant of the:
questions.explanation: Ravi used his imagination to create a royal and ancient lineage for the simple kitten.

questions.text: Why did Ravi have to drink most of the milk he brought for the kitten?
options: To prove to Paati that he wasn't feeding anything else
questions.explanation: Paati saw him with the tumbler, so he had to pretend it was for himself to keep the kitten a secret.

questions.text: What was the strange 'kreeching' sound Mridu heard?
questions.explanation: Lalli was taking music lessons from a master and was struggling to play properly.

questions.text: How did the music master look?
options: Bony figure with a bald head and a fringe of oiled black hair
questions.explanation: The story provides a detailed and somewhat humorous description of the master's physical appearance.

questions.text: The music master's notes seemed to float up and settle into the visible tracks of the:
questions.explanation: The master's playing was perfect, unlike Lalli's 'derailing' attempts.

questions.text: Who was the 'venerable' guest who visited the garden every day for a week?
questions.explanation: A beggar had been coming to the house regularly, but Rukku Manni wanted him to find another house.

questions.text: The beggar showed his feet to the children. What did they see?
options: Pink peeling blisters on the soles of his bare feet
questions.explanation: The hot tar road had burnt his bare feet, causing painful blisters.

questions.text: Why did the children decide to give the beggar a gift?
options: Out of pity for his blistered feet
questions.explanation: They felt he needed chappals to walk on the burning hot road.

questions.text: Whose chappals did the children finally give to the beggar?
questions.explanation: The children found a pair of old-looking, dusty chappals in the veranda and gave them away.

questions.text: What was the music master's reaction when he couldn't find his chappals?
options: He was upset and called them brand new
questions.explanation: He claimed they were expensive and new, although the children saw them as old and grey.

questions.text: Who did Rukku Manni suspect had taken the chappals initially?
questions.explanation: She saw the children's guilty faces and realized they were involved.

questions.text: To solve the problem, Rukku Manni gave the music master ________ chappals.
questions.explanation: She gave him Gopu Mama's relatively new chappals as a replacement.

questions.text: How did the music master feel about the replacement chappals?
options: He was very happy but tried to hide it
questions.explanation: His eyes lit up because they were better than his old ones, but he pretended they were just 'alright'.

questions.text: Rukku Manni laughed at the end because she wondered what ________ would say when he came home.
questions.explanation: Gopu Mama was in the habit of taking off his shoes and putting on chappals immediately after coming home.

questions.text: The theme of the story 'A Gift of Chappals' is:
options: Innocent generosity and the contrast between adult and child perspectives
questions.explanation: It highlights how children act on pure compassion, sometimes confusing adults.

questions.text: The word 'scrawny' used for Lalli's neck means:
questions.explanation: It is used to describe her lean physical appearance while playing the violin.

questions.text: Which city is the story set in?
questions.explanation: Mridu lives with her grandparents in Madras.

questions.text: Ravi compared the music master's violin playing to a ________ going smoothly on tracks.
questions.explanation: He contrasted the master's smooth melody with Lalli's erratic playing.

questions.text: Where was the kitten keeping itself hidden?
options: In a torn football lined with sacking
questions.explanation: They found a clever hiding spot in the backyard inside an old football.

questions.text: What was the music master wearing on his finger?
questions.explanation: A gold ring glinted on his hand as his fingers moved up and down the violin.

questions.text: The beggar had been leaning against a ________ tree in the garden.
questions.explanation: He made himself at home under the neem tree before being asked to leave.

questions.text: What did the beggar do after receiving the music master's chappals?
options: He pushed his feet into them and vanished quickly
questions.explanation: He left hurriedly before anyone could change their mind or realize whose they were.

questions.text: Who is the author of 'A Gift of Chappals'?
questions.explanation: Vasantha Surya is the writer of this story, originally from 'Mridu in Madras'.

questions.text: Rukku Manni's initial reaction to the children giving away the chappals was:
questions.explanation: She was cross because they gave away someone else's property without asking.

questions.text: Ravi defended his action by comparing himself to ________ who gave his own skin.
questions.explanation: Ravi cited the legend of Karna's generosity to justify giving away the chappals.

questions.text: The music master's chappals were described by him as ________.
questions.explanation: He wanted to emphasize the loss to get a good replacement or compensation.

questions.text: Which of these words describes Lalli's violin playing?
questions.explanation: The story uses the word 'derailing' to show how badly she was playing.

questions.text: Ravi complained that it is hard to be ________ to animals because elders always scream.
questions.explanation: He felt a conflict between what elders teach (be kind) and how they act (don't bring animals inside).

questions.text: Mridu's grandmother's name is:
questions.explanation: Tapi is the name, and Paati is the Tamil word for grandmother.

questions.text: The music master had a 'bone-deep' look, meaning he was very ________.
questions.explanation: It emphasizes his lanky and skinny physical frame.

questions.text: The children's 'gift' to the beggar caused a ________ in the household.
questions.explanation: It led to a search for the missing chappals and tension with the music master.

questions.text: What does the word 'venerable' mean?
questions.explanation: It is often used for something old and respected (used ironically for the beggar's 'tenure' at the house).

questions.text: The Pallava emblem Ravi mentioned was a ________.
questions.explanation: Ravi linked the kitten's appearance to the lion emblem of the ancient Pallava kings.

questions.text: Why did the music master say 'My chappals were brand new'?
options: To increase their value in the eyes of others
questions.explanation: He was being dishonest to make his loss seem much greater than it was.

questions.text: Gopu Mama usually ________ as soon as he comes home.
options: Throws off his shoes and socks
questions.explanation: This habit made Rukku Manni realize he would immediately miss his chappals.

questions.text: The beggar blessed the children for their:
questions.explanation: The chappals were the most valuable thing they could have given him in that heat.

questions.text: At the end, Rukku Manni felt that Gopu Mama would be ________ with her decision.
questions.explanation: She knew she would have to face his reaction for giving his chappals to the music master.
